title: dianthus pyrenaicus subsp. catalaunicus
intro: Dianthus pyrenaicus subsp. catalaunicus, also known as the Pyrenean pink, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family. It is native to the mountainous regions of the Pyrenees and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The health benefits of Dianthus pyrenaicus subsp. catalaunicus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote a good night's sleep. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create remedies for various health conditions.
